skill.name: example-skill
skill.description: Demonstrates the structure, sections, and documentation patterns used to create a well-formed AI skill.
skill.summary: Reference example for creating clear, structured AI skill documentation.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code spans, weak cryptography keywords, and system reconnaissance text. Manual review found no executable commands, cryptographic operations, reconnaissance behavior, or prompt injection attempts in the referenced files. The skill is safe to publish with a low-risk note because the static findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown inline code references and documentation paths. These lines do not execute shell commands or define executable skill behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each referenced line is plain Markdown documentation. I found no shell execution syntax or tool instruction that would run these paths as commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive text and documentation references. The reviewed lines contain no hashing algorithm use, encryption implementation, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are frontmatter or prose about skill structure. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detection points to a documentation bullet about anti-patterns. The line does not request host inspection, environment discovery, or system command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is instructional prose about documenting mistakes to avoid. I found no reconnaissance command or semantic request to collect system information.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear examples when creating reusable AI skills. This skill provides a compact reference for sections, workflows, examples, and supporting files.
actual_capabilities: Shows a complete skill frontmatter example with metadata fields.
Explains when to use a skill and how to describe its purpose.
Provides a simple workflow for applying a skill to a task.
Lists best practices and anti-patterns for skill documentation.
Includes a reference guide for required and optional skill sections.
limitations: It is an example skill, not a domain-specific production workflow.
It does not include scripts, executable automation, or generated assets.
One referenced examples file is mentioned in prose but is not present.
It does not validate skill packages or enforce schema rules automatically.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Skill
use_cases.description: Use the example structure to outline a new skill before adding specialized instructions.

use_cases.title: Review Documentation Quality
use_cases.description: Compare a draft skill against the documented sections, examples, and anti-patterns.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Use the compact guide to explain how skill documentation is organized.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Required Sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a draft skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to list the required sections for my draft skill and explain what each section should contain.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Skill Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill as a reference and create an outline for a new skill about my domain. Include purpose, workflow, examples, best practices, and anti-patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Skill Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my skill draft with this example skill. Suggest clearer wording, missing sections, and any anti-patterns I should fix.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this example skill and its structure guide to review my skill for marketplace readiness. Focus on clarity, scope, examples, bundled resources, and user expectations.

output_examples.input: A user asks for the required parts of a skill document.
output: A concise checklist of required sections.
A short explanation of what each section should achieve.
Notes about optional references, scripts, and assets.

output_examples.input: A user provides a draft skill that lacks examples.
output: Specific missing example areas are identified.
The response suggests practical usage scenarios.
The response flags broad or unclear documentation patterns.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one clear domain or capability.
Start with when to use the skill before listing detailed workflows.
Include examples, best practices, anti-patterns, and helpful references.
anti_patterns: Do not create a skill that tries to cover unrelated tasks.
Do not skip trigger conditions or expected usage scenarios.
Do not reference bundled files unless they are actually included.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It demonstrates the structure and documentation style for creating an AI skill.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain documentation only and do not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documented as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include reusable templates?
faq.answer: It references a template path, but the packaged files only include the skill document and structure guide.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production automation?
faq.answer: No. It is a reference example for documentation, not an automation skill.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing a skill based on it?
faq.answer: Check scope, required sections, included resources, examples, limitations, and security review results.
